Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Sorry, I didn't understand the original problem that this patch was fixing. 

I think using "-0" for this option is confusing, because "find -0" and "lfs find -0" have a specific meaning - terminate printed strings with an explicit '\0' so that tools like "xargs -0" can handle pathnames with embedded spaces, newline, etc.

It would be better to make the handling here consistent with the other tools so that "lfs fid2path -0" is printing a NUL-terminated filename suitable for "xargs -0" usage instead of just skipping the '\n' completely, since that essentially prints the wrong filename, and could potentially conflict with a *different* filename that doesn't have the embedded newline in it.

LGTM. I didn't know file names could have newlines. That's good to know.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1
> 
> LGTM. I didn't know file names could have newlines. That's good to know.

Yes, in fact Linux allows all bytes except the NUL byte (\x00) and slash character '/', so this feature is massively going to help with parsing fid2path output! It's unsafe to attempt it without this.

Thanks to Arshad for implementing this, when I didn't have time to contribute the Gerrit changes myself!

> Thanks to Arshad for implementing this, when I didn't have time to contribute the Gerrit changes myself!

Simon, there was alredy a patch by you attached to this ticket. Which I have copied. :-) So, thanks for the patch. Notice, I have included you in "Signed-off-by". However, my observation over time is that, nothing would move even a little, if there is no review. So double thanks to Andreas for timely review. If this patch does see a merge to master (which it will), it would be because of his timely review (PS: which he does in middle of midnight :-) ).

> > LGTM. I didn't know file names could have newlines. That's good to know.

Short Answer. Lustre is POSIX compliant.

parents.subject: LU-16949 lnet: get monitor thread to update ping buffer

commit.subject: LU-16605 lfs: Add -0 option to fid2path
commit.message:
LU-16605 lfs: Add -0 option to fid2path

Currently fid2path adds '\n' after printing
pathnames. Add '-0' option to fid2path to
add NUL('\0') at the end after printing out
pathnames instead of '\n'. This allows
pathnames that contain newlines('\n') to be
correctly interpreted by binaries like xargs.

Without -0 option:
$ lfs fid2path /mnt/lustre 0x200000401:0x1:0x0
/mnt/lustre/Test
_file
/mnt/lustre/Link_
file

With -0 option:
$ lfs fid2path -0 /mnt/lustre 0x200000401:0x1:0x0 | xargs --null
/mnt/lustre/test
_file /mnt/lustre/link_
file

Test-case sanity/226e added.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity
Reported-by: Simon Westersund <simon.westersund@csc.fi>
Signed-off-by: Simon Westersund <simon.westersund@csc.fi>
Signed-off-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Change-Id: I9e3e32cde6c6abe83df48afd191ec167c74ac7e6
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51736
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
